_README: Operator policy template for the Radar 'Policy' tab. The live policy is stored
in Postgres (governance.operator_policy); paste an edited copy here and Save.
RISK-TIER FLOW (the main feature — agents AND tools):
1. OPA assigns a BASELINE risk tier first — the OPA agent's SLM tiers each
tool call; an agent's baseline is its authoritative (declared+magazine) tier.
2. This JSON's 'risk' field UPDATES that tier (operator override / relabel).
3. The updated tier is APPLIED against the slider ('allowed risk level' on Tool
Risk Tiers): tier BELOW the slider -> allowed; AT OR ABOVE -> denied.
So: OPA baseline -> your 'risk' -> slider decides. Set 'risk' lower to admit
something under the slider, higher to push it over.
EXPLICIT OVERRIDE (optional): 'allow' beats the slider entirely —
'allow': true  -> always allowed,  'allow': false -> always denied
(use it for a hard pre-execution deny on dangerous tools). Omit 'allow' to let
the risk-tier + slider flow decide.
Omitting a field — or the string "default" — means 'no opinion, use the OPA
baseline / slider'. Keys match an agent id/name or a tool name (globs ok).
Agent risk -> low|medium|high ; tool risk -> low|medium|high|severe. '_'-keys ignored.
SKILLS (optional): match any AGENT by a skill its AgentCard declares (shown in the
Policy tab's Skills column). Because skills are SELF-declared, a skill rule may only
TIGHTEN: 'allow': false to deny, or 'risk' to RAISE the agent's tier. It can never
allow or lower (an agent must not whitelist itself by claiming a skill). A rule that
names an agent directly (in 'agents') always wins over a skill rule.
note: risk-tier overrides: OPA baseline -> JSON risk -> slider

agent-langgraph-*.note: override magazine tier -> recognised under the slider

web_search.note: OPA tier -> relabel low -> below slider -> allowed

run_command.note: shell exec - severe tier + hard deny

external_post.note: any agent that CAN post outbound -> deny

run_command.note: any agent that CAN run shell -> raise its risk tier
